tasks.title: Install AI-first engineering harness baseline
tasks.notes: Adds a restartable AI-first harness and minimal Go CLI scaffold so make verify is deterministic before product implementation begins.

tasks.title: Define schema/versioning and artifact descriptor registries
tasks.notes: Pinned config_version, JSON schema family rules, artifact type registry, descriptor canonicalization, semantic descriptors, and artifact version IDs.

tasks.title: Define runner discovery and plugin installation semantics
tasks.notes: Pinned resolution order, plugin manifest shape, MVP out-of-band installation rule, diagnostics, and capability negotiation.

tasks.title: Define security model and conformance scenarios
tasks.notes: Pinned core/runner trust boundary, path safety, confidence blocking, and MVP fake-runner conformance scenarios.

tasks.title: Implement project config and resource parser
tasks.notes: Reads fs_project.yml, requires config_version: 1, loads resource YAML, validates names, paths, artifact aliases, source/ref syntax, asset/eval/policy refs, and emits structured parse diagnostics.

tasks.title: Implement manifest graph and selectors
tasks.notes: Builds manifest resources, parent/child maps, resource IDs, selector support for name/tag/path/resource_type/parent/child, selector unions, and deterministic manifest JSON.

tasks.title: Implement artifact descriptors and safe path handling
tasks.verification_override_reason: No conformance target exists yet; descriptor and path-safety behavior is covered by Go tests until the suite is added in FBT-MVP-016.
tasks.notes: Computes file and canonical directory SHA-256 descriptors, maps artifact aliases, generates artifact_version IDs, normalizes project-relative paths, and rejects path/symlink escapes.

tasks.title: Implement local state store, locks, and atomic writes
tasks.verification_override_reason: No conformance target exists yet; local state locking and atomic write behavior is covered by Go tests until the suite is added in FBT-MVP-016.
tasks.notes: Writes manifest/state/run_results/artifact_versions/evaluation_results/policy_decisions with schema metadata, lock files, append-only run results, idempotent artifact version writes, and atomic snapshot updates.

tasks.title: Implement planner and dirty-state semantics
tasks.notes: Selects runnable transforms, computes dirty reasons for source/assets/policy/eval/runner/model/effective/output changes, and reports blocked confidence requirements.

tasks.title: Implement plan and artifact CLI surfaces
tasks.notes: Turns planned plan/artifact commands into working inspection commands with human and JSON output, stable exit codes, and fixture-backed tests. Public parse/state commands were removed by FBT-UNIX-013, and plan was made read-only by FBT-UNIX-016.

tasks.title: Implement runner discovery and diagnostics
tasks.notes: Resolves project runners, project/user plugin manifests, and PATH conventions. Runner readiness now surfaces through `fbt doctor`; the older runner subcommands were removed by FBT-UNIX-013.

tasks.title: Implement JSON-RPC runner protocol client
tasks.verification_override_reason: No conformance target exists yet; protocol behavior is covered by fake-runner Go tests until the suite is added in FBT-MVP-016.
tasks.notes: Starts runner processes, negotiates initialize, sends fbt/runTransform, reads JSONL notifications, handles output candidates, context cancellation, and protocol errors.

tasks.title: Implement fake and command runners for local MVP
tasks.verification_override_reason: No conformance target exists yet; fake and command runner behavior is covered by process-level Go tests until the suite is added in FBT-MVP-016.
tasks.notes: Provides no-service fake runner and the command runner now promoted to adapters/command for deterministic local transforms without adding heavy dependencies to the base CLI.

tasks.title: Implement build lifecycle and idempotent commit
tasks.verification_override_reason: No conformance target exists yet; build lifecycle and idempotent clean rerun behavior is covered by Go tests and CLI smoke until the suite is added in FBT-MVP-016.
tasks.notes: Runs the first parse-plan-run-commit-state lifecycle, manages .fbt/work, validates output candidate containment, records transform_run and artifact_version, and keeps clean reruns idempotent.

tasks.title: Implement policy and security enforcement
tasks.verification_override_reason: No conformance target exists yet; policy and failed-commit safety behavior is covered by Go tests until the suite is added in FBT-MVP-016.
tasks.notes: Enforces write scopes, output work-dir containment, artifact_path containment, secret redaction helpers, timeout/output limits, and failed-run state safety for denied commits.

tasks.title: Implement evals and confidence
tasks.verification_override_reason: No conformance target existed when this task landed; current behavior is covered by Go tests, CLI smoke, and conformance.
tasks.notes: Runs deterministic evals before commit, records evaluation results, and grants confidence for downstream requirements. Earlier review/approval behavior from this task was removed by FBT-UNIX-011.

tasks.title: Implement AI-native runner experience
tasks.verification_override_reason: No conformance target or real-provider opt-in smoke exists yet; optional local LLM/agent runner behavior is covered by protocol Go tests and a local build test until broader suites are added in FBT-MVP-016.
tasks.notes: Adds optional out-of-process local LLM and agent runner examples with usage/cost/provenance and redacted tool-call reporting, passes model/tools through build, records usage/provenance in run results, and keeps provider SDKs outside the base runtime.

tasks.title: Implement init templates and runnable knowledge-loop example
tasks.notes: Implements fbt init with blank/support/knowledge_ops/incident templates, adds committed examples/knowledge_ops, and verifies an init -> plan -> build -> downstream build loop without external services.

tasks.title: Implement diff and artifact lineage inspection
tasks.notes: Adds raw text and Markdown heading-aware diff plus `fbt diff` with previous/current-version resolution. The older built-in docs generator was removed from the public CLI by FBT-UNIX-013; standard exports and artifact inspection are the supported surfaces.

tasks.title: Complete MVP conformance, packaging, and release smoke
tasks.notes: Adds executable conformance scenarios, local release binary dist check, and wires conformance/dist checks into make verify alongside init -> doctor -> plan -> build -> artifact -> export smoke coverage.

tasks.title: Stamp MVP version and release metadata
tasks.notes: Replace 0.0.0-dev with the first MVP versioning flow, support build-time version injection, update dist version checks, and document the release version contract.

tasks.title: Prepare public repository remote and signed release baseline
tasks.notes: Remote is configured for github.com:nyuta01/fbt, non-interactive ls-remote succeeds, and SSH signing is configured locally for the release point. Existing history is kept intact; release-baseline commits and the v0.1.0 tag are signed from this point forward.

tasks.title: Publish MVP release artifacts and checksums
tasks.notes: Published https://github.com/nyuta01/fbt/releases/tag/v0.1.0 with darwin/linux/windows amd64/arm64 archives, SHA256SUMS, and version metadata. Local checksum verification passed, and GitHub verify workflows for main and v0.1.0 completed successfully.

tasks.title: Update documentation from draft to MVP-ready status
tasks.notes: Replace draft/scaffold language with MVP-ready status, make the quick start reflect implemented commands, document local knowledge-loop usage, and keep non-goals/limitations explicit.

tasks.title: Capture user-facing UX hardening backlog
tasks.notes: Converted user-perspective research against DVC, dbt, Dagster, Bazel, MLflow, Terraform, Pulumi, and kubectl into prioritized post-MVP UX backlog tasks.

tasks.title: Show actionable next steps for blocked and skipped work
tasks.notes: When plan/build reports blocked or skipped artifacts, include the concrete unblock command or next inspection command. User research analogs: DVC status/repro, dbt build skip behavior, and Terraform/Pulumi preview/apply flows make the next safe action explicit.

tasks.title: Add artifact explanation command for plan decisions
tasks.notes: Add a user-facing explanation surface such as `fbt explain <artifact>` or `fbt plan --why` that reports why an artifact will run, skip, or block, including upstream dependencies, dirty reasons, confidence constraints, and previous run evidence. User research analogs: Bazel query somepath/allpaths and Dagster asset selection/lineage.

tasks.title: Add artifact path, show, and history commands
tasks.notes: Make generated output easy to find and inspect with commands such as `fbt artifact path <id>`, `fbt artifact show <id>`, and `fbt artifact history <id>`. Include artifact version, logical path, immutable storage path, runner/model, confidence, and generating run. User research analogs: DVC list/get/artifacts get --show-url, MLflow artifacts list/download, and Dagster asset catalog metadata.

tasks.title: Superseded review inspection with artifact inspection
tasks.notes: Original review command work was removed by FBT-UNIX-011. The supported inspection surface is now `fbt artifact show`, `fbt artifact history`, `fbt artifact explain`, and `fbt diff`.

tasks.title: Add top-level project doctor command
tasks.notes: Add `fbt doctor` as a single readiness check for project config, runner discovery, runner protocol initialization, target/state writability, and policy-relevant path constraints. User research analog: dbt debug checks project setup, local configuration, system environment, dependencies, and connection readiness.

tasks.title: Improve project validation and YAML authoring diagnostics
tasks.notes: Strengthen project validation output with line-oriented, actionable YAML diagnostics in `fbt doctor`, `fbt plan`, and `fbt build`; consider narrow scaffolding for new transforms. User research analogs: Terraform validate, kubectl explain, and dbt parse/debug reduce config-authoring ambiguity before execution.

tasks.title: Improve CLI command framework and human output
tasks.notes: Migrates command/flag/help handling to Cobra while preserving fbt's strict command surface. Human output for plan/build/artifact now uses concise status labels, aligned summaries, short resource names, and explicit Details sections instead of leading with full internal IDs.

tasks.title: Align human CLI output columns
tasks.notes: Keeps Glamour out of the default status output because the issue is structured row alignment, not Markdown rendering. Aligns summary, key/value, dependency, and output rows using fixed-width helpers and text/tabwriter while keeping --json unchanged.

tasks.title: Capture lineage standard export backlog
tasks.notes: Converted standard-format investigation into post-MVP tasks. Current lineage is fbt-native JSON; OpenLineage should be the first lineage export target, OpenTelemetry should cover execution traces and GenAI telemetry, OpenMetadata should be evaluated as a catalog integration, and graph/trace/catalog visualization should be delegated to standard-compatible tools rather than a custom fbt UI.

tasks.title: Define standard lineage, telemetry, and visualization export contracts
tasks.notes: Specify the command surface and stable mapping for exporting fbt-native lineage without replacing internal schemas. Cover artifact/artifact_version, transform, transform_run, source, eval, policy, runner/model metadata, redaction rules, JSON output shape, and the explicit non-goal of building a custom lineage graph UI when standard-compatible viewers can consume the export.

tasks.title: Implement Marquez-compatible OpenLineage export for artifact lineage
tasks.notes: Implements `fbt export openlineage [--output PATH]` as OpenLineage RunEvent NDJSON. Maps fbt artifacts/artifact_versions to datasets, transforms to jobs, transform_runs to UUID-backed runs, and confidence, eval, descriptor, model, and policy metadata to `fbt_` custom facets so Marquez/OpenLineage tooling can own visualization.

tasks.title: Add OpenTelemetry-compatible execution telemetry export
tasks.verification_override_reason: No network OTel backend is required for default verification; OTLP/JSON payload shape and CLI export are covered by Go tests and smoke.
tasks.notes: Implements `fbt export otel [--output PATH]` as local-first OTLP/JSON trace export. Build invocations and transform runs become spans, safe runner events become span events, and usage, cost, model, artifact, and policy metadata become attributes without adding a default network exporter.

tasks.title: Evaluate OpenMetadata catalog export
tasks.notes: Evaluated OpenMetadata catalog integration and decided against a base `fbt export openmetadata` command. The supported path is `fbt export openlineage` plus external OpenMetadata OpenLineage ingestion; direct OpenMetadata publishing remains an optional integration outside core.

tasks.title: Add conformance fixtures for standard exports
tasks.notes: Adds deterministic standard-export checks to `tests/conformance/run.sh`: generated OpenLineage and OTel payloads must contain expected standard keys, fbt facets/attributes, UUID-shaped OpenLineage run IDs, runner span events, and must exclude raw source content plus a redaction marker.

tasks.title: Document standard visualization integrations
tasks.verification_override_reason: Manual Marquez, Jaeger, Tempo, and Grafana backend smokes are intentionally outside the default local gate; docs link official integration paths and `make verify` validates references.
tasks.notes: Adds user-facing recipes for visualizing fbt lineage and telemetry through standard tools: OpenLineage events in Marquez for artifact/job/run lineage, OTLP/JSON traces in Jaeger, and Tempo/Grafana via a Collector or Alloy pipeline. Keeps fbt's own docs/explain surfaces lightweight and avoids implementing a custom graph UI.

tasks.title: Add opt-in real LLM runner smoke
tasks.verification_override_reason: `make real-llm-smoke` is intentionally skipped unless `FBT_REAL_LLM_RUNNER_COMMAND` is set; default verification covers the script and docs without network/provider credentials.
tasks.notes: Adds `make real-llm-smoke` and `scripts/smoke-real-llm.sh`, an environment-gated smoke that creates a temporary project and runs `doctor -> build -> artifact show` against an external stdio JSON-RPC LLM runner command without adding provider SDKs or network access to `make verify`.

tasks.title: Decide and close run/debug command surface
tasks.notes: Closed the MVP command surface by removing `run` and `debug` placeholders from CLI help/docs. `build` remains the execution command; diagnostics stay under `doctor`, `plan`, `build`, and `artifact`; former placeholders now return the normal unknown-command error.

tasks.title: Expand conformance beyond compact shell smoke
tasks.notes: Expanded the deterministic conformance matrix with schema error checks, clean rerun skip behavior, docs/export redaction, standard export determinism, dirty-state propagation after asset changes, and existing policy-denied commit safety without adding external services.

tasks.title: Emit full policy decision records during lifecycle
tasks.notes: Build now records policy decisions for allowed and denied commit checks, including check messages, candidate artifact version IDs, and decision timestamps. Generated docs include policy decisions, run results reference decision IDs, OTel export includes decision ID attributes, and conformance checks allowed/denied records.

tasks.title: Implement semantic descriptors for common document types
tasks.notes: Adds first-pass `text_normalized_v1` and `markdown_ast_v1` semantic descriptors for text/Markdown artifacts, persists them on artifact versions, and surfaces them in artifact inspection and standard exports while keeping raw descriptor digests as artifact version identity.

tasks.title: Capture external runner hardening backlog
tasks.notes: Captures the post-MVP runner extensibility gap: fbt core should stay provider/runtime-free, while OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Codex, Claude Code, and other integrations should be easy to add as out-of-process protocol runners.

tasks.title: Pass complete execution context to protocol runners
tasks.notes: Build now populates `fbt/runTransform` with resolved source inputs, current artifact-version inputs, descriptors, semantic descriptors, transform assets, runner config metadata, prior/current state, and plan dirty reasons so external runners do not need to parse fbt project files themselves.

tasks.title: Harden runner process invocation with args, env, and cwd
tasks.notes: Adds runner `args` and optional `cwd` to project config and plugin manifests, passes configured args/cwd/env to runner processes, filters env passthrough to base variables plus declared names, and reports missing declared env names without printing values.

tasks.title: Enforce runner capability negotiation in doctor and build
tasks.notes: Makes runner `initialize` capabilities authoritative for protocol version, transform types, output artifact types, and output-candidate support. Build fails before transform execution on incompatibility, and `fbt doctor` reports the same diagnostics.

tasks.title: Define safe CLI-agent adapter contract for Codex and Claude Code
tasks.notes: Defines the safe adapter contract for wrapping existing CLI agents behind the fbt stdio protocol, requiring staging workspaces, fail-closed policy translation, redacted events, and output candidates under work.outputs. Go and conformance tests verify outside-work candidates are rejected before official commit.

tasks.title: Add runner authoring kit and protocol conformance fixtures
tasks.notes: Adds a language-neutral runner authoring guide, JSON protocol fixtures, a reusable black-box runner conformance harness, and `make runner-conformance`; the strict fixture now runs in `make verify` against the source fake runner.

tasks.title: Clarify demo runners and external runner UX
tasks.notes: Renames generated support/incident runner identities and wrappers to `demo.llm`, `demo.agent`, and `bin/fbt-demo-*-runner`, updates bundled demo runner metadata and checked-in examples, and documents the replacement path through runner conformance, project runner config, doctor, and plan.

tasks.title: Define optional provider adapter packaging outside core
tasks.notes: Documents optional provider and CLI-agent adapter package conventions, project config, plugin manifests, PATH behavior, credential boundaries, versioning, and conformance checks for `fbt-runner-openai`, `fbt-runner-anthropic`, `fbt-runner-gemini`, `fbt-runner-codex-cli`, and `fbt-runner-claude-code` without adding SDKs to fbt core.

tasks.title: Add practical manual generation examples
tasks.notes: Adds external-runner, production-shaped examples for incident logs to incident response runbook and support inquiry/response logs to support resolution manual. The examples include realistic source records, output format assets, policies, deterministic section evals, and a plan smoke that runs without provider calls.

tasks.title: Improve examples for first-time users
tasks.notes: Adds an examples index, rewrites all example READMEs around user intent, input evidence, output artifacts, credential boundaries, command outcomes, repeated source growth, and generated receipts. Source fingerprints now include resolved file sets and file content so adding files under glob sources makes dependent transforms dirty, and mixed-status doctor diagnostics no longer mark executable runner checks as errors.

tasks.title: Add daily QA operations example
tasks.notes: Adds an offline daily QA operations example that uses stable Markdown directory sources under data/qa/inbox, multiple source artifacts, and multiple outputs under latest logical paths. The practical examples smoke now builds the daily candidate artifacts and promoted manual update without fbt-native review.

tasks.title: Add optional OpenAI Responses runner
tasks.verification_override_reason: Real OpenAI API calls require a user-provided secret and are intentionally outside `make verify`; runner behavior is covered by an httptest Responses API fixture plus parse/plan example smoke.
tasks.notes: Adds an optional source-checkout `adapters/openai` external runner that speaks fbt stdio JSON-RPC, reads `OPENAI_API_KEY`, calls the OpenAI Responses API, writes output candidates under `work.outputs`, and is wired into the practical examples through project-local wrappers.

tasks.title: Add Folio-inspired documentation site
tasks.notes: Adds an Astro/Starlight documentation site modeled after Folio's docs structure, with intro, get-started, CLI, runners, lineage/standards, and reference sections. GitHub Pages deployment and docs-site build are wired into the verification gate.

tasks.title: Refresh README using Folio structure
tasks.notes: Reworks the top-level README after Folio's public README pattern: centered identity, badges, short product definition, surfaces, docs, quickstart, install table, examples, lineage, releases, and repository harness.

tasks.title: Ground README and docs in actual user workflow
tasks.notes: Adds user-first docs grounded in a captured support quickstart, including actual command outputs, generated files, artifact excerpts, lineage/export outputs, and a standard-export graph image. Also fixes generated demo runner wrappers so the documented quickstart works from outside the source checkout and guards that with e2e smoke.

tasks.title: Remove ambiguous quickstart diagram
tasks.notes: Removes the ambiguous support-loop graphic from README and docs-site entry pages. The quickstart is now explained through actual command output, generated files, and standard export commands instead of an invented product diagram.

tasks.title: Define quickstart scope
tasks.notes: Defines quickstart as a small control-plane acceptance demo rather than a support-product demo, model-quality benchmark, realistic manual-generation workflow, or architecture walkthrough. Adds lifecycle-stage explanation and per-step intent text.

tasks.title: Clarify README product story
tasks.notes: Rewrites README as a product entry point: purpose, problem, concrete support-manual example, what fbt owns in the workflow, current capabilities, quickstart fixture, project structure, boundaries, install, and docs.

tasks.title: Expand README concrete example
tasks.notes: Adds representative source record content, response-log content, transform YAML, required output sections, output path, and fbt lineage responsibility to the README's concrete support manual example before showing commands.

tasks.title: Simplify README mental model
tasks.notes: Reframes README around the core user model: sources plus instructions plus an external runner produce a generated artifact plus a local build receipt. The support example now starts from the support lead's problem, shows the non-reusable evidence, generated manual, receipt, controlled process, and then the recipe/commands.

tasks.title: Replace README example with incident runbook workflow
tasks.notes: Replaces the README support-manual example with a simpler incident-notes-to-runbook workflow, removes YAML from the example, explains the recipe as a small table, and states what each command gives the user.

tasks.title: Explain README commands as checkpoints
tasks.notes: Rewrites the README example command sequence as user checkpoints: preview, generate, inspect, export, and explain, with each step stating what fbt does and what the user gets. Adds actual plan output and shortened offline quickstart lifecycle output.

tasks.title: Register Unix-style product backlog
tasks.notes: Registers a Unix-philosophy backlog that keeps fbt focused on one job: external tools generate files while fbt records, explains, versions, and exports the artifact lifecycle.

tasks.title: Clarify the single-purpose product boundary
tasks.notes: Clarifies that fbt is the build receipt, version, and lineage control plane for generated file artifacts. It composes with, but does not replace, dbt, DataChain, DVC, Snakemake, remark, Pandoc, schedulers, provider SDKs, artifact stores, or metadata catalogs.

tasks.title: Make artifact explanation the primary UX
tasks.notes: Elevates `fbt artifact explain` with a decision sentence, dependency fingerprints, upstream artifact state, current/previous run context, dirty or blocked reasons, and next commands. `plan` now includes a build next-step for run nodes.

tasks.title: Define stable source-window operations
tasks.notes: Documents stable inbox/latest paths, cumulative versus new-items-only windows, external partitioning and readiness checks, and keeps scheduling outside core. Practical examples smoke appends a new Markdown question under the stable inbox and asserts the dependent transform becomes dirty.

tasks.title: Add existing-tool runner examples for remark and Pandoc
tasks.notes: Adds command-transform argv support, a markdown_toolchain example wrapping remark-style and Pandoc-style scripts through the command runner, and practical smoke coverage. The example is deterministic offline while documenting where real remark and pandoc calls belong.

tasks.title: Add dbt and DataChain interoperability examples
tasks.notes: Added examples/data_tool_interop, a runnable command-transform example that consumes dbt run artifacts and DataChain job outputs as fbt sources, produces a versioned Markdown brief, and is covered by practical examples smoke.

tasks.title: Provide a minimal runner adapter scaffold
tasks.notes: Added examples/runner_adapter_scaffold with a dependency-free Python JSON-RPC runner, plugin manifest, docs, and make runner-scaffold-conformance coverage in make verify.

tasks.title: Complete composable graph selection UX
tasks.notes: Implemented shared transform selection for `+target`, `target+`, and `+target+`; plan/build now expand through the resource graph and return transform IDs only. Added graph tests, ambiguity errors, CLI docs, and knowledge-loop smoke coverage.

tasks.title: Define explicit rebuild and cache controls
tasks.notes: Added `plan --force` and `build --force` as the minimal explicit rebuild control. Force marks selected clean transforms dirty with `forced rebuild`, does not bypass blocking or policy checks, and reuses existing immutable artifact versions for identical output.

tasks.title: Keep semantic evaluation outside core
tasks.notes: Clarified that MVP core runs deterministic evals only; `semantic` and `llm_judge` declarations are recorded as skipped and grant no confidence. Added docs and examples that model-based judges should be external runner transforms producing judge report artifacts.

tasks.title: Use standard visualization backends in examples
tasks.notes: Added examples/standard_visualization and rewrote standard visualization docs around reproducible OpenLineage/OTel export plus Marquez and OTLP HTTP ingestion recipes. The docs now require screenshots to come from real standard backends, not custom fbt diagrams.

tasks.title: Remove built-in review from core
tasks.notes: Removed fbt-native review/approval behavior so core stays a file build tool. Human approval and publishing are documented as external workflows; smoke coverage asserts `fbt review` is not a command.

tasks.title: Make CLI argument handling strict
tasks.notes: Unknown command flags, unsupported global flags, extra positional arguments, and selectors that match no transforms now fail instead of being ignored. Build selection now supports the same selector forms as plan, keeping the primary CLI predictable and scriptable.

tasks.title: Prune public CLI command surface
tasks.notes: Removed public parse/eval/docs/state/runner command routing and the duplicate artifact versions alias. Parsing, evals, state writes, and runner diagnostics now stay behind doctor, plan, and build, while tests and smokes assert the pruned commands are unknown.

tasks.title: Remove stale non-core feature references from specs
tasks.notes: Audited specs and active plans for stale docs/review/approval/debug command-surface claims. Current docs now use docs-site build, artifact inspection, confidence/upstream blocking, and OpenLineage/OTel export language; remaining hits are explicit outside-core or superseded historical notes.

tasks.title: Converge value narrative on the build receipt
tasks.notes: Converges README, usage guide, CLI reference, design doc, docs site, and examples on the build-receipt model. Keeps `build` as the execution command because fbt treats generated files as build outputs: selected artifacts plus immutable versions, checks, local state, and standard-exportable lineage.

tasks.title: Clarify or remove plan side effects
tasks.notes: Made `fbt plan` read-only by removing its manifest write. Manifest snapshots are written by `fbt build`; CLI tests, smoke checks, dist checks, and docs now assert or describe the read-only plan behavior.

tasks.title: Capture critical product gap backlog
tasks.notes: Converted the product audit into restartable backlog items. The highest-priority gaps are one-invocation DAG builds, failed-run receipts, inert config cleanup, strict YAML diagnostics, CLI-agent adapter safety, and stale boundary-doc cleanup.

tasks.title: Execute selected build DAGs in dependency order
tasks.notes: Selected transforms now plan and build in dependency order. Missing upstream artifacts no longer block a downstream transform when the upstream producer is selected in the same invocation; upstream run intent propagates dirty state to downstream nodes, and build rechecks runtime blockers before each transform.

tasks.title: Persist failed build and transform receipts
tasks.notes: Failed builds now append invocation_completed and transform_run receipts with safe error kind/message for runner setup, capability, protocol, output-contract, policy, eval, and cancellation failures. Failed receipts do not advance artifact versions or current artifact pointers, and OTel exports include failed spans plus exception events.

tasks.title: Remove or implement inert config fields
tasks.notes: Reserved execution.max_workers, execution.fail_fast, defaults.cache, defaults.confidence, and transform-level cache with CONFIG_FIELD_RESERVED diagnostics and removed the no-op fields from public config structs, manifest transform resources, examples, and project-config docs.

tasks.title: Reject unknown YAML fields with actionable diagnostics
tasks.notes: Project and resource YAML now reject unknown fields with YAML_FIELD_UNKNOWN diagnostics that include file, line, resource when available, and a hint. Conformance covers misspelled top-level, runner, source, transform, policy, and eval fields while preserving documented project aliases and free-form meta/config/contract objects.

tasks.title: Add opt-in real runner adapter smoke matrix
tasks.notes: Keep provider SDKs outside core, but make OpenAI, Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, and internal CLI adapters easier to validate through opt-in smoke commands and documented adapter package contracts.

tasks.title: Harden CLI-agent adapter safety contract
tasks.notes: Adapters for Codex CLI, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, or other agents must prove staging-workspace behavior, policy fail-closed mapping, redaction, and no direct writes to sources, artifact paths, or .fbt/state.

tasks.title: Define local state and artifact retention hygiene
tasks.notes: Immutable history is valuable, but daily high-volume projects need a clear local retention and archive story. Define the smallest safe hygiene mechanism without adding a metadata database or remote artifact store.

tasks.title: Add opt-in standard backend visualization verification
tasks.notes: Base verify should stay service-free, but docs should be backed by opt-in Marquez and OTLP backend smoke recipes plus screenshots captured from real standard tools.

tasks.title: Remove stale core-boundary references
tasks.notes: Audit current-state docs for stale claims such as core-owned approval state, removed CLI commands, docs generation, or internal debug surfaces. Add a deterministic grep guard for the non-core terms where possible.

tasks.title: Tighten CLI help and command descriptions
tasks.notes: Root, artifact, export, plan, build, and retention help now describe what each command returns. Global --select and --state-dir help explains command applicability and local state versus immutable artifact storage.

tasks.title: Make common CLI errors actionable
tasks.notes: Declared-but-unbuilt artifacts, empty selectors, and --dry-run now emit strict errors plus short Hint lines with concrete next actions such as fbt build --select TARGET, fbt plan, or using plan instead of --dry-run.

tasks.title: Make next commands copy-paste safe
tasks.notes: When users invoke fbt with --project-dir or a non-default --state-dir, next commands printed by plan, build, and artifact explain now preserve that context so the command works from the same shell location.

tasks.title: Show committed artifact paths after build
tasks.notes: Successful build output now shows each committed artifact's logical output path, committed version, and contextual artifact show command so users immediately know where the generated file landed.

tasks.title: Make human artifact details scannable
tasks.notes: Human artifact show now summarizes semantic descriptors instead of printing dense one-line JSON, and retention shows human-readable state/artifact sizes while preserving exact byte counts for JSON automation.

tasks.title: Clarify local state and artifact directory controls
tasks.notes: The relationship among --state-dir, .fbt/state, .fbt/artifacts, artifact_path, and state.backend is now explicit. Unsupported state backends and escaping state.path values fail with actionable parser diagnostics.

tasks.title: Simplify runner terminology and onboarding
tasks.notes: OpenAI, Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, scripts, converters, and internal tools are now presented through one external-command runner mental model. Adapter, protocol, conformance, and smoke terminology remains available for authors.

tasks.title: Clarify runner adapter repository layout
tasks.notes: The ambiguous top-level runners directory was removed. Source-checkout runner adapter examples now live under examples/runner_adapters, while protocol-only fake fixtures live under tests/runner_fixtures.

tasks.title: Clarify standard export command UX
tasks.notes: export openlineage and export otel keep stdout raw for piping and print clearer --output summaries with standard format, output path, record count, and backend handoff guidance.

tasks.title: Group doctor runner diagnostics for scanability
tasks.notes: Human doctor output now groups diagnostics into Project, State, and Runners sections with runner-specific nesting, while --json preserves the flat checks array for automation.

tasks.title: Rework README first-user journey
tasks.notes: README now follows a first-user journey: value, command questions, project anatomy, offline first loop, real workflow example, tool boundaries, and goal-oriented deep links.

tasks.title: Make README source-to-artifact examples concrete
tasks.notes: README now maps concrete support and incident files to source declarations, instruction assets, transform recipes, runners, artifacts, checks, and receipts so first-time users can see what actually becomes what.

tasks.title: Show README input-to-output content snippets
tasks.notes: README now shows source content excerpts next to generated artifact excerpts for the offline support loop and the real incident runbook workflow.

tasks.title: Research official runner adapter package design
tasks.notes: Research report recommends official runners as external adapter packages with conformance, checksums, docs, security policy, and version compatibility, while keeping provider SDKs and agent runtimes out of fbt core.

tasks.title: Adopt monorepo adapter module strategy
tasks.notes: Official adapters will start in this repository as nested modules under adapters/, with provider-free SDK code under sdk/ and the root go.mod kept core-only.

tasks.title: Create provider-free Go runner SDK module
tasks.notes: Adds provider-free sdk/go nested module with runner protocol types, stdio JSON-RPC server helpers, output-candidate helpers, redaction helpers, go.work, and make verify SDK coverage.

tasks.title: Promote command runner into official adapter module
tasks.notes: Promotes the command runner into official nested module adapters/command with SDK-based protocol handling, manifest, README, tests, conformance target, and updated practical example wrappers.

tasks.title: Promote OpenAI runner into official adapter module
tasks.notes: Promotes the OpenAI Responses runner into official nested module adapters/openai with SDK-based protocol handling, manifest, README, httptest coverage, network-free conformance, and updated practical example wrappers.

tasks.title: Add official Codex CLI and Claude Code adapter modules
tasks.notes: Adds official nested Codex CLI and Claude Code adapter modules with SDK-based protocol handling, staging workspaces, fail-closed agent safety markers, fixture CLI tests, and strict agent-adapter conformance.

tasks.title: Fix CLI-agent live invocation issues
tasks.notes: Live adapter testing fixed conformance fixture model forwarding, added bounded CLI output in errors, and corrected Claude Code prompt handling so real CLI failures are actionable.

tasks.title: Fix OpenAI live conformance model handling
tasks.notes: OpenAI adapter no longer forwards conformance fixture model names to the real Responses API. Live OpenAI conformance and a temporary incident runbook build passed with a real API key.

tasks.title: Enforce output-size limits for directory artifacts
tasks.notes: Directory descriptors now record aggregate regular-file bytes. max_output_bytes applies to file and directory artifacts, and build regressions prove denied directory outputs do not advance current artifact pointers.

tasks.title: Make official adapter modules remotely installable
tasks.notes: Official adapter go.mod files no longer contain local SDK replace directives. Each adapter records the VCS-resolved sdk/go dependency, and `make adapter-install-smoke` verifies clean `go install module@commit` for all official adapter commands through a temporary bare VCS remote.

tasks.title: Enforce CLI-agent policy mapping instead of marker-only safety
tasks.notes: Codex CLI and Claude Code adapters now map supported fbt policy controls into CLI flags and fail closed before external CLI invocation when a requested policy cannot be represented. Positive and negative agent-adapter conformance runs cover both adapters.

tasks.title: Remove JSON-RPC JSONL scanner size limits
tasks.notes: Core protocol client and SDK server now set an explicit 16 MiB JSONL frame limit. Tests cover messages above Go scanner's default token size, and the protocol spec documents the boundary.

tasks.title: Fail visibly on CLI-agent staged input truncation
tasks.notes: Codex CLI and Claude Code adapters now copy staged source and asset files completely when within the explicit 2 MiB per-file limit, and fail before external CLI invocation with an actionable oversized-file error when the limit is exceeded.

tasks.title: Expose runner stderr and exit diagnostics in protocol failures
tasks.notes: The protocol client now captures bounded redacted stderr and exit status for startup and protocol-call failures. Build receipts and CLI errors include safe diagnostics and a runner setup hint without leaking configured runner env values.

tasks.title: Remove stale review-gates language from docs site assets
tasks.notes: The public docs OG image no longer says `review gates`, and the drift check now scans public docs assets for stale current-state review/approval phrases.

tasks.title: Make manual-generation docs show concrete source-to-artifact evidence
tasks.notes: Manual-generation docs, practical example READMEs, and the reference examples now show representative source contents, prompt/format assets, the configured runner, generated artifact paths, expected artifact excerpts, and artifact explain receipts.

tasks.title: Strengthen runner and standards docs with runnable examples
tasks.notes: Runner, OpenAI adapter, authoring contract, lineage, standard export, visualization, and project-config docs now include runnable commands, minimal YAML snippets, conformance output, doctor diagnostics, export summaries, and concrete file pointers.

tasks.title: Make the first own-files success path self-service
tasks.notes: README, docs-site quickstart/manual-generation docs, and the first own-files guide now show how to replace sample source files and instructions, prove the loop with demo runners, inspect receipts, and switch to an external runner. `make verify` now includes `own-files-smoke`.

tasks.title: Harden official adapter install and live verification UX
tasks.notes: Added `make official-adapter-smoke` for fixture/fake official adapter checks and documented a three-level path: fixture/fake protocol smoke, clean adapter install smoke, and opt-in installed/live adapter smoke.

tasks.title: Document daily high-volume source operations patterns
tasks.notes: Added daily source operations guide and `daily-ops-smoke` to prove stable source windows, multiple source directories, multiple artifacts, day-two source descriptor changes, and external scheduler boundaries.

tasks.title: Add external semantic and evidence-quality check examples
tasks.notes: `examples/semantic_eval_boundary` is now runnable. It builds a manual artifact, runs an external command transform that writes an evidence quality report, and verifies that fbt records the report as a normal artifact with lineage while judge logic stays outside core.

tasks.title: Simplify end-user install path for core and adapters
tasks.notes: Install and release docs now cover core archive checksum verification, source builds, separate official adapter install commands, adapter version pinning expectations, and source/installed/live verification commands.

tasks.title: Validate retention guidance with a high-volume fixture
tasks.notes: Added `retention-high-volume-smoke`, which creates eight artifact versions, verifies current/historical retention counts, checks JSON archive roots, and confirms the read-only archive guidance for `.fbt/state` plus `.fbt/artifacts`.

tasks.title: Run real-workflow pilots and capture user friction
tasks.notes: Completed real workflow pilots for daily QA operations and external evidence-quality reports. Added `docs/pilots/2026-05-29-real-workflow-pilots.md` with observed commands/results/friction, and updated `examples/README.md` so copied examples declare `FBT_SOURCE_ROOT` explicitly. `make verify` passed.

tasks.title: Move product conformance scenarios from shell to a structured harness
tasks.notes: Moved product conformance scenarios into `tests/conformance/run.py`, kept `tests/conformance/run.sh` as a thin compatibility wrapper, and updated `make conformance` to call the structured harness. `make conformance`, the wrapper, and `make verify` passed.

tasks.title: Generate JSON Schema for project configuration
tasks.notes: Added generated schemas for `fs_project.yml` and resource YAML under `schemas/`, generator/check script `scripts/generate-project-config-schema.py`, docs links, and `make project-config-schema-check` inside `make verify`.

tasks.title: Define adapter release tags, checksums, and signature workflow
tasks.notes: Documented module-scoped adapter tags, signed tag verification, Go module checksum verification, future binary `SHA256SUMS`/`cosign sign-blob` flow, and release metadata. Added `.github/workflows/release-adapters.yml` and `make adapter-release-plan-check`; `make verify` passed.

tasks.title: Document OS sandbox execution profiles for high-security use
tasks.notes: Added `docs/security/os-sandbox-profiles.md`, docs-site security profile page, security spec and adapter docs updates, plus `make security-profiles-check` inside `make verify`. OS process/network isolation remains external to fbt core.

tasks.title: Reconcile spec statuses and remaining implementation questions
tasks.notes: Core spec status labels now reflect the implemented MVP baseline, broad stale remaining-question sections were replaced with explicit post-MVP boundaries, validate-docs now checks the new section, and quality-score references no longer point at this completed cleanup task.

tasks.title: Guard quality score next-task references against completed tasks
tasks.notes: Added a `harness-check` guard that validates task IDs referenced from `docs/QUALITY_SCORE.md`, fails unknown task references, and fails references to completed tasks. Updated the quality-score methodology and current Harness PDCA row. `make verify` passed.

tasks.title: Design optional runner and adapter lockfile semantics
tasks.notes: Added `docs/runner-lockfile-spec.md`, `schemas/fbt-lock-v1.schema.json`, docs references, and `make runner-lockfile-spec-check`. The contract defines optional validator-only `fbt.lock.json` semantics, doctor/build expectations, dirty-state participation, conformance expectations, and explicit no package-manager behavior.

tasks.title: Require explicit policy for agent transforms
tasks.notes: `type: agent` transforms now fail parsing with `AGENT_POLICY_MISSING` when `policy` is omitted. Parser tests and structured conformance cover the negative case, and security/project-config docs plus docs-site snippets state that agent transforms require explicit policy.

tasks.title: Make skipped semantic and LLM-judge evals visible in user-facing UX
tasks.notes: Skipped semantic/LLM-judge evals now persist reason and hint fields, build output and transform-run receipts show the skipped state, artifact explain marks delegated eval dependencies as skipped, and docs/schema text points users to external judge transforms for active gates.

tasks.title: Maintain self-PDCA loop
tasks.notes: Standing self-PDCA loop checked after MVP task execution; quality score, active plans, feature list, and progress handoff are current and guarded by make verify.

tasks.title: Maintain permanent-fix loop
tasks.notes: Standing permanent-fix loop checked after MVP task execution; no repeated active failure required a new guard, and failure-log invariants remain enforced by make verify.
